src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.general_idea: Remove extension of the deprecated WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter class.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.why_exists: WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter is deprecated in Spring Security 5.7+ and removed in Spring Security 6.0 (required for Spring Boot 3/Java 21). Extending it causes compilation errors or runtime failures in the target environment.

@EnableGlobalMethodSecurity is removed in Spring Security 6.0. Without @EnableMethodSecurity, method-level security annotations like @Secured or @PreAuthorize will not function, potentially causing security bypasses or compilation errors if the old annotation was present.

src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.general_idea: Replace the deprecated configure(HttpSecurity) method override with a SecurityFilterChain bean definition.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.before: protected void configure(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception { ... }
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.after: public SecurityFilterChain configure(HttpSecurity http) throws Exception { ... }
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.why_exists: The configure(HttpSecurity) method in WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter is removed in Spring Security 6.0. Security configuration must now be defined via a SecurityFilterChain bean to compile and run.

src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.general_idea: Replace the deprecated authorizeRequests() API with the new authorizeHttpRequests() API.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.why_exists: authorizeRequests() is removed in Spring Security 6.0. Using it results in a compilation error. The new API uses a lambda-based registry for request matching.

src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.general_idea: Replace the deprecated addFilterBefore with the new filter registration syntax if applicable, or update filter class references.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.why_exists: While addFilterBefore still exists, the context of the filter (RequestHeaderAuthenticationFilter vs UsernamePasswordAuthenticationFilter) and the bean injection style change. More critically, the old filter bean definition is removed and replaced, requiring the new bean to be injected and registered correctly to avoid runtime security failures.

src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.general_idea: Replace deprecated .disable() calls on httpBasic, csrf, formLogin, logout with AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.why_exists: The .disable() method on these configurers is removed in Spring Security 6.0. Calling it causes a compilation error. The new API requires passing a consumer (like AbstractHttpConfigurer::disable) to the configurer method.

src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.general_idea: Remove the configure(WebSecurity) method and move ignore rules to the SecurityFilterChain.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.before: public void configure(WebSecurity web) throws Exception { web.ignoring() ... }
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.why_exists: The configure(WebSecurity) method is removed in Spring Security 6.0. Ignoring requests must now be handled via permitAll() in the SecurityFilterChain or by not matching them in the filter chain at all. Failing to migrate this causes compilation errors.

src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.general_idea: Update session management configuration to use the new lambda-based API.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/configu.why_exists: The fluent API for sessionManagement() without a consumer is deprecated/removed in favor of the lambda-based configuration in Spring Security 6.0. This change ensures compilation compatibility.

src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/reposit.general_idea: Replace Spring Data JPA repository interface with a manual class using jOOQ for database access.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/reposit.before: public interface UserRepository extends JpaRepository<User, String> {
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/reposit.after: public class UserRepository { ... private final DSLContext jooqDsl; ... }
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/reposit.why_exists: The original code relies on Spring Data JPA interfaces and JPA entities which are being removed or replaced; without this change, the application fails to compile due to missing JpaRepository and User entity references, and fails to run due to missing JPA infrastructure.

src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/rest/Au.general_idea: Update validation import from javax.validation to jakarta.validation for Jakarta EE 9+ compatibility.
src/main/java/ch/zkmf2024/server/rest/Au.why_exists: javax.validation package does not exist in Jakarta EE 9+ (Spring Boot 3 / Java 21 stack), causing compile error.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade Spring Boot parent from 2.6.2 to 3.2.0 to support Java 21 and Jakarta EE namespaces.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 2.x does not support Java 21 as a baseline and uses javax.* namespaces, causing compile errors with Java 21/Jakarta EE 9+ APIs.

pom.xml.general_idea: Update the Java version property from 17 to 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: The project targets Java 21; compiling with Java 17 settings would fail to utilize or enforce Java 21 features and compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace javax.mail and javax.activation with Spring Boot Starter Mail to align with Jakarta EE namespaces in Spring Boot 3.
pom.xml.why_exists: Spring Boot 3 requires Jakarta Mail (jakarta.mail.*); the old javax.mail artifact is incompatible and will cause runtime linkage errors or compile failures if code references Jakarta namespaces.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace MySQL connector with PostgreSQL driver to match the new database backend.
pom.xml.why_exists: The application connects to a PostgreSQL database; using the MySQL driver will result in runtime connection failures.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace Testcontainers MySQL module with PostgreSQL module to match the new database backend.
pom.xml.why_exists: Integration tests require a PostgreSQL container; using the MySQL container will cause test failures due to schema/driver mismatch.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace Spring Data JDBC/JPA/REST starters with Web, Validation, WebFlux, and jOOQ starters to reflect architectural changes.
pom.xml.why_exists: The application code has been rewritten to use jOOQ and WebFlux/Web MVC; the old Spring Data starters are unused and their removal prevents classpath conflicts and reduces build size.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace Firebase Admin SDK with Apache POI for Excel processing.
pom.xml.why_exists: The application no longer uses Firebase for storage/auth but now generates Excel files; the old dependency is unused and the new one is required for compilation.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace old JWT libraries with JJWT (io.jsonwebtoken) to support modern JWT handling.
pom.xml.why_exists: The application code uses JJWT classes; without this dependency, compilation fails due to missing classes.
